description: Indicates the 'absolute' value of the maximum allowed acquisition frame rate. The 'absolute' value is a float value that indicates the maximum allowed acquisition frame rate in frames per second given the current settings for the area of interest, exposure time, and bandwidth.

name: ResultingLineRate

name: ActionGroupMask

CounterReset can be used to reset the Counter independently from the CounterResetSource. To disable the counter temporarily, set CounterEventSource to Off.

name: RegionMode
description: Controls if the selected Region of interest is active and streaming.

name: RegionSelector
description: Selects the Region of interest to control. The RegionSelector feature allows devices that are able to extract multiple regions out of an image, to configure the features of those individual regions independently.

name: ReverseScanDirection
description: select scan direction reverse or not.

name: ReverseX
description: Flip horizontally the image sent by the device. The AOI is applied after the flip.

name: ReverseY
description: Flip vertically the image sent by the device. The Region of interest is applied after the flipping.

name: SelectBitRegion
description: Selects the Sensor Bit Region of interest. Selects 8-Bits Region from 12 Bits Sensor Region.

name: SensorDigitizationTaps
description: Number of digitized samples outputted simultaneously by the camera A/D conversion stage.

name: SensorTaps
description: Number of taps of the camera sensor.

name: TestPattern
description: Selects the type of test pattern that is generated by the device as image source.

name: TestPatternGeneratorSelector
description: Selects which test pattern generator is controlled by the TestPattern feature.

name: Width
description: Width of the image provided by the device (in pixels).

name: WidthMax
description: Maximum width of the image (in pixels).

name: LUTEnable
description: Activates the selected LUT.

name: LUTIndex
description: Control the index (offset) of the coefficient to access in the selected LUT.

name: LUTSelector
description: Selects which LUT to control.

name: LUTValue
description: Returns the Value at entry LUTIndex of the LUT selected by LUTSelector.

name: ActivateShading
description: Activates the selected shading set.

name: LSCCRCEnable
description: Enable the LSC Control

name: LSCSTATEnable
description: Statistics generate LSC coefficients.

name: NUCEnable
description: Enable the FPNC and PRNUC Control

name: PRNUCTargetB
description: Set PRNUC Target B

name: PRNUCTargetEnable
description: Enable - use PRNUC target R/G/B; DISABLE - use statistical mean value.

name: PRNUCTargetG
description: Set PRNUC Target G

name: PRNUCTargetR
description: Set PRNUC Target R

name: ShadingSelector
description: Selects the kind of shading correction.

name: TransferAbort
description: Aborts the streaming of data Block(s). The current block transmission will be completed. This feature must be available when the TransferControlMode is set to "UserControlled.

name: TransferBlockCount
description: Specifies the number of data Blocks that the device should stream before stopping. This feature is only active if the TransferOperationMode is set to MultiBlock.

name: TransferControlMode
description: Selects the control method for the transfers.

name: TransferOperationMode
description: Selects the operation mode of the transfer.

name: TransferPause
description:
Pauses the streaming of data Block(s). Pausing the streaming will immediately suspend the ongoing data transfer 
        even if a block is partially transfered. The device will resume its transmission at the reception of a TransferResume command.

name: TransferQueueCurrentBlockCount
description: Returns the number of Block(s) currently in the transfer queue.

name: TransferQueueMaxBlockCount
description: Controls the maximum number of data blocks that can be stored in the block queue of the selected stream.

name: TransferQueueMode
description: Specifies the operation mode of the transfer queue.

name: TransferQueueOverFlowCount
description: Returns the number of Count(s) Over Flow in the transfer queue.

name: TransferResume
description: Resumes a data Blocks streaming that was previously paused by a TransferPause command.

name: TransferSelector
description: Selects which stream transfers are currently controlled by the selected Transfer features.

name: TransferStart
description:
Starts the streaming of data blocks out of the device. This feature must be available 
        when the TransferControlMode is set to "UserControled". If the TransferStart feature is not writable (locked), 
        the application should not start the transfer and should avoid using the feature until it becomes writable again.

name: TransferStop
description: Stops the streaming of data Block(s). The current block transmission will be completed. This feature must be available when the TransferControlMode is set to "UserControlled.

name: GevCCP
description: Controls the device access privilege of an application.

name: GevCurrentDefaultGateway
description: Indicates the default gateway IP address to be used on the given network interface.

name: GevCurrentIPAddress
description: Current IP address for the given network interface.

name: GevCurrentIPConfigurationDHCP
description: Indicates if DHCP IP configuration scheme is activated on the given network interface.

name: GevCurrentIPConfigurationLLA
description: Indicates if Link Local Address IP configuration scheme is activated on the given network interface.

name: GevCurrentIPConfigurationPersistentIP
display_name: GEV Current IP Configuration Persistent IP
description: Indicates if Persistent IP configuration scheme is activated on the given network interface.

name: GevCurrentSubnetMask
description: Current subnet mask of the given interface.

name: GevDeviceModeCharacterSet
description: Character set used by all the strings of the bootstrap registers.

name: GevDeviceModeIsBigEndian
display_name: GEV Device Mode Is Big Endian
description: Endianess of the device registers.

name: GevFirstURL
description: The first choice of URL for the XML device description file.

name: GevGVCPHeartbeatDisable
description: Disables the GVCP heartbeat.

name: GevGVCPPendingAck
description: Enables generation of PENDING_ACK.

name: GevGVCPPendingTimeout
description: Indicates the longest GVCP command execution time before a device returns a PENDING_ACK.

name: GevGVSPExtendedIDMode
description: Enables the extended IDs mode.

name: GevHeartbeatTimeout
description: Indicates the current heartbeat timeout in milliseconds.

name: GevIEEE1588
description: Enables the IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol to control the timestamp register.

name: GevIEEE1588Status
description: Provides the status of the IEEE 1588 clock.

name: GevInterfaceSelector
description: Selects which physical network interface to control.

name: GevLinkSpeed
description: Indicates the speed of transmission negotiated by the given network interface in Mbps.

name: GevMACAddress
description: MAC address of the network interface.

name: GevMCDA
description: Controls the destination IP address for the message channel.

name: GevMCPHostPort
description: Controls the port to which the device must send messages. Setting this value to 0 closes the message channel.

name: GevMCRC
description: Controls the number of retransmissions allowed when a message channel message times out.

name: GevMCSP
description: This feature indicates the source port for the message channel.

name: GevMCTT
description: Provides the transmission timeout value in milliseconds.

name: GevMessageChannelCount
description: Indicates the number of message channels supported by this device.

name: GevNumberOfInterfaces
description: Indicates the number of physical network interfaces supported by this device.

name: GevPAUSEFrameReception
description: Controls whether incoming PAUSE Frames are handled on the given logical link.

name: GevPersistentDefaultGateway
description: Indicates the persistent default gateway for this network interface. It is only used when the device boots with the Persistent IP configuration scheme.

name: GevPersistentIPAddress
description: Indicates the persistent IP address for this network interface. It is only used when the device boots with the Persistent IP configuration scheme.

name: GevPersistentSubnetMask
description: Indicates the persistent subnet mask associated with the persistent IP address on this network interface. It is only used when the device boots with the Persistent IP configuration scheme.

name: GevSCDA
description: Indicates the destination IP address for this stream channel.

name: GevSCPD
description: Indicates the delay (in timestamp counter units) to insert between each packet for this stream channel. This can be used as a crude flow-control mechanism if the application or the network infrastructure cannot keep up with the packets coming from the device.

name: GevSCPDirection
description: Transmit or Receive of the channel

name: GevSCPHostPort
description: Host port of the channel

name: GevSCPInterfaceIndex
description: Index of network interface to use.

name: GevSCPSBigEndian
description: Endianess of multi-byte pixel data for this stream.

name: GevSCPSDoNotFragment
description: The state of this feature is copied into the "do not fragment" bit of the IP header of each stream packet.

name: GevSCPSFireTestPacket
description: Sends a test packet.

name: GevSCPSPacketSize
description: Specifies the stream packet size (in bytes) to send on this channel.

name: GevSCSP
description: Indicates the source UDP port address for this stream channel.

name: GevSecondURL
description: The second choice of URL to the XML device description file.

name: GevStreamChannelCount
description: Indicates the number of stream channels supported by this device.

name: GevStreamChannelSelector
description: Selects the stream channel to control.

name: GevSupportedOption
description: Indicates whether or not the selected GEV option is supported.

name: GevSupportedOptionSelector
description: Selects the GEV option to interrogate for existing support.

name: GevTimestampControlLatch
description: This command latches the current timestamp value of the device.

name: GevTimestampControlLatchReset
description: This command resets the timestamp control latch.

name: GevTimestampControlReset
description: This command resets the timestamp value for the device

name: GevTimestampTickFrequency
description: Indicates the number of timestamp ticks in 1 second (frequency in Hz).

name: GevTimestampValue
description: This is a read only element. It indicates the latched value of the timestamp.  (The timestamp must first be latched using the Timestamp Control Latch command.)

name: GevVersionMajor
description: Major version of the specification.

name: GevVersionMinor
description: Minor version of the specification.

name: PacketUnorderSupport
description: Is Support Packet out of order.

name: PayloadSize
description: Number of bytes transferred for each image or chunk on the stream channel.

name: UserSetCurrent
description: Indicates the user set that is currently in use.  At initialization time, the camera loads the most recently saved user set.

name: UserSetDefault
description: This enumeration sets the configuration set to be used as the default startup set. The configuration set that has been selected as the default startup set will be loaded as the active set whenever the camera is powered on or reset.

name: UserSetLoad
description: This command loads the selected configuration set from the non-volatile memory in the camera to the volatile memory and makes the selected set the active configuration set. Once the selected set is loaded, the parameters in the selected set will control the camera.

name: UserSetSave
description: This command copies the parameters in the current active configuration set into the selected user set in the camera's non-volatile memory.

name: UserSetSelector
description: This enumeration selects the configuration set to load, save or configure. Possible values for the User Set Selector are: Default: Selects a configuration set that contains factory settings. User Set 1: Selects the first user set. When the Default configuration set is selected and loaded using User Set Load, the device must be in default factory settings state and must make sure the mandatory continuous acquisition use case works directly. Default User Set is read-only and cannot be modified.
